chapter_context.summary: Values a good name over riches, encourages training children early, warns against borrowing slavery, and highlights the blessings of diligent workmanship.

outline.text: A Good Name Preferred, Rich and Poor Meet, and Train Up a Child
outline.explanation: This section focuses on A Good Name Preferred, Rich and Poor Meet, and Train Up a Child (22:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Borrowing Slavery, Sowing Inequity, Bountiful Eye, and Slothful Lion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Borrowing Slavery, Sowing Inequity, Bountiful Eye, and Slothful Lion (22:7-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Exhortation to Hear Words of Wise and Trust in LORD
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Exhortation to Hear Words of Wise and Trust in LORD (22:17-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Robbing Poor, Angry Companions, Surety, and Diligent Work
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Robbing Poor, Angry Companions, Surety, and Diligent Work (22:22-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 22, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
